rationale.R1.3: The agent output does not check claim-evidence linkage, as the evaluation reports in-distribution accuracy on standard test sets without addressing out-of-distribution robustness.
rationale.RX.1: All output presented as equally confident, with no uncertainty disclosure for clearly out-of-reach claims or missing evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope with one drift (the medical-imaging/autonomous-driving framing), but the experiments do not test deployment distribution differences from training.
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by making unsubstantiated claims about out-of-distribution robustness and safety-critical applications.
rationale.RX.5: The output blends sources without traceability, as the mechanism is asserted but not specified mathematically in the abstract/intro.
